perturbation.original_text: All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party. Within ten (10) days following the receipt of a written request referencing this Agreement and this paragraph from either party furnishing Information hereunder, the receiving party will del iver to the furnishing party all tangible materials containing or embodying the Information received fr om the furnishing party, except for materials containing Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilations, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receivin g party or its representatives, together with a certificate executed by the receiving party certify ing that all such materials in the receiving party' s
perturbation.changed_text: All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party. Within ten (10) days following the receipt of a written request referencing this Agreement and this paragraph from either party furnishing Information hereunder, the receiving party will not deliver the information. It doesn't matter that the materials containing Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilations, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receivin g party or its representatives, together with a certificate executed by the receiving party certify ing that all such materials in the receiving party' s
perturbation.explanation: The modification directly contradicts the original agreement. The original text mandates the receiving party to return tangible materials containing the information within ten days of a written request. The changed text explicitly states that the receiving party will *not* deliver the information, disregarding the clause for exceptions like incorporated analyses. This creates a direct conflict in the obligations of the receiving party, making the contract unenforceable on this point, which creates a contradiction in section 8.0, where it states that the receiving party *will* deliver to the furnishing party all tangible materials.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership; Return of Information No license to a party, under any trademark, patent, copyright, mask work protection right, or any other intellectual property right, is either grante d or implied by the conveying of Information to suc h party. All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party. Within ten (10) days following the receipt of a written request referencing this Agreement and this paragraph from either party furnishing Information hereunder, the receiving party will not deliver the information. It doesn't matter that the materials containing Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilations, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receivin g party or its representatives, together with a certificate executed by the receiving party certify ing that all such materials in the receiving party' s 3 - Revised May 7, 2010 possession have been delivered to the furnishing pa rty or destroyed. That portion of the Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilat ions, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its representati ves shall be held by the receiving party and kept confidential as provided above, or shall be destroy ed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text state that the receiving party *will* deliver tangible materials within ten days. The changed text explicitly states that the receiving party *will not* deliver the information. This creates a direct and significant conflict within the contract, thus a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damage s would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that the other party s hall be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or forestall any such br each or threatened breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or i n equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damages would be the exclusive and sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that the other party shall not be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or forestall any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedy shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, superseding all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause (9.0) allows for injunctive relief in addition to monetary damages for breaches of the agreement. The modified clause states that only money damages are allowed and no other remedies are available. This change directly contradicts the original intention of allowing for equitable relief, it leads to an in-text contradiction in remedies available for a breach of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies for Breach Each party understands and agrees that money damages would be the exclusive and sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that the other party shall not be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or forestall any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedy shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, superseding all other rights and remedies available at law or i n equity.
perturbation.justification: The original clause (9.0) allows for injunctive relief and money damages for breaches of the agreement. The modified clause states that only money damages are allowed and no other remedies are available. The contradicted text states the same modified text. Because the modified text negates the availability of injunctive relief whereas the original clause allowed for it, there is a clear contradiction in remedies available for a breach of the contract, and an in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate the exchange of Informat ion under this Agreement at any time by written notice to the other specifically referencing this A greement. In any event, however, the obligations o f each party to maintain the confidentiality of the I nformation it has received under this Agreement shall continue for the later to occur of (i) a peri od of three (3) years after such termination, or (i i) if this Agreement is incorporated by reference into another agreement between the parties, the term, including extensions, of that agreement, plus three (3) years.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate the exchange of Informat ion under this Agreement at any time by written notice to the other specifically referencing this A greement. In any event, however, the obligations o f each party to maintain the confidentiality of the I nformation it has received under this Agreement shall cease immediately upon such termination, this superceeds anything within the subclauses.
perturbation.explanation: The change directly contradicts the original terms regarding confidentiality after termination. Initially, confidentiality obligations extended for three years post-termination (or longer if incorporated into another agreement). With this change, confidentiality obligations cease immediately upon termination. This creates a significant uncertainty because it creates contradiction in 11.0 on what are the terms and obligations after terminating the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term; Termination Either party may terminate the exchange of Informat ion under this Agreement at any time by written notice to the other specifically referencing this A greement. In any event, however, the obligations o f each party to maintain the confidentiality of the I nformation it has received under this Agreement shall cease immediately upon such termination, this superceeds anything within the subclauses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text in a way that directly contradicts the original intent regarding confidentiality obligations after termination. The original text states that confidentiality obligations continue for a period of three years after termination (or longer if incorporated into another agreement), whereas the changed text explicitly states that these obligations cease immediately upon termination and this superceeds any subclauses. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, so the contradiction is not whether the change caused a contradiction to another part of the document, but whether the change itself created a contradiction within the agreement, and it has. The explanation also states this. This creates a clear and direct conflict, as one part of the contract now states one thing, and another (the same) section states the exact opposite. The contradiction falls under the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction since the key obligations regarding confidentiality after termination are defined inconsistently, creating uncertainty and making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified, supplemented, o r amended orally, but only by a writing signed by both parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified, supplemented, o r amended orally, and a writing signed by both parties hereto is optional.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause (13.0) strictly prohibits oral modifications to the agreement, requiring any changes to be in writing and signed by both parties. The modified clause explicitly allows for oral modifications and makes written amendments optional. This introduces a critical point of ambiguity and contradiction because it impacts the validity of the contract, which makes oral modification allowed. Contradiction in 13.0 where it discusses amendments to the current contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Amendment This Agreement may be modified, supplemented, o r amended orally, and a writing signed by both parties hereto is optional.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement may be modified orally, and a written agreement is optional. This directly contradicts the original text, which requires all modifications to be in writing and signed by both parties. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, but the change created a contradiction with what it originally stated (in the original text).
